kill_fact: The board's load-bearing dated catalyst already FIRED AND FAILED: the FDA issued a Complete Response Letter on Jan 30, 2026 (confirmed by company 8-K/press release). The dossier's '2026-09-30 FDA approval decision' is FICTIONAL - no such agency action date exists. It is merely the company's guided Q3-2026 NDA RESUBMISSION window (Class 2, undated within the quarter, NOT yet filed as of 2026-06-29), which only starts a fresh ~6-month clock. The genuine re-decision is ~Q1-2027, two-to-three quarters out and two gates away (clean resubmission acceptance, then review). So there is NO 105-day binding binary - this is a SOFT_EXTENDED, second-swing-at-the-same-regulator FDA bet, exposed to another CRL on the Class 2 resubmission. The ~2.5:1 asymmetry to a genuinely funded ~$2.75 floor survives verification, but it is a 2027 payoff, and the imminence the aggressive-entry slot requires is absent.
corrections: (1) catalyst_status is SOFT_EXTENDED, not a hard/dated catalyst: the 2026-09-30 'decision' is a placeholder, not an FDA action date - delete it. Real sequence: CRL fired Jan 30 2026 -> Type A meeting Mar 30 2026 (FDA aligned on PK + human-factors study designs, NO new efficacy trials, AdCom NOT required) -> planned Q3-2026 Class 2 resubmission (undated, not yet filed) -> FDA re-decision ~Q1-2027. (2) Effective approval probability must be discounted for resubmission-slip risk + second-CRL risk + the undated extra quarters; there is no clock-dated decision to attach high odds to in-window. (3) The $150M Oaktree facility is TRANCHED/CONDITIONAL (~$55M refi at close; +$20M only on FDA approval, +$25M on sales milestones, +$50M by mutual consent) - the full $150M is NOT unconditionally available. The floor rests on ~$110M Q1 cash + $75M RTW + a growing base business (Q1 rev $14.4M +66% YoY, no going-concern flag), which is adequate but FUNDAMENTAL/revenue-backed, NOT contractual deal cash - breachable on a second CRL or royalty miss.
evidence: Aquestive 8-K / press release (event 2026-01-30, filed 2026-02-02): FDA issued a Complete Response Letter for Anaphylm (dibutepinephrine) Sublingual Film; deficiencies limited to human-factors validation + a single PK study + packaging/labeling, NO CMC/efficacy/comparability concerns; company expects to resubmit 'as early as the third quarter of 2026'. https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/0001398733/000139873326000005/anaphylmcrlpressrelease2.htm

Aquestive 8-K / press release (2026-03-30): completed in-person Type A meeting with FDA, aligned on PK + human-factors study designs and on labeling vs. new clinical trials, FDA acknowledged revised container-opening mechanism, final minutes expected early May 2026, REAFFIRMED Q3-2026 NDA resubmission guidance - no PDUFA/Class-2 decision date assigned. https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1398733/000139873326000022/typeameetingpressrelease.htm

Aquestive Q1 2026 results 8-K (filed May 2026): revenue $14.4M (+66% YoY), license/royalty $5.4M (from $0.8M), ~$110M cash at quarter-end, new $150M Oaktree facility (tranched: ~$55M refi up front, +$20M on FDA approval, +$25M sales milestone, +$50M mutual consent) + $75M RTW, projects >$150M liquidity at launch, no going-concern language. https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/0001398733/000139873326000029/q12026-ex991earningsrelease.htm

Aquestive Investor Relations releases confirming the Jan-30-2026 CRL with the narrow fixable deficiency set, and a separate release that the FDA will NOT require an Advisory Committee meeting for the Anaphylm NDA (reduces AdCom-surprise risk on the resubmission path). https://investors.aquestive.com/news-releases/news-release-details/aquestive-therapeutics-announces-fda-issuance-complete-response
